_comment: Source: NASEMSO National Model EMS Clinical Guidelines v3.0 (March 2022). Resuscitation: Do Not Resuscitate Status/Advance Directives/Healthcare Power of Attorney (POA) Status. NASEMSO national provisional base.
level_display: All Levels — Interventions Scoped Per Provider Level
condition: Do Not Resuscitate Status/Advance Directives/Healthcare Power of Attorney (POA) Status
protocol_reference: NASEMSO National Model EMS Clinical Guidelines v3.0 — Resuscitation: Do Not Resuscitate Status/Advance Directives/Healthcare Power of Attorney (POA) Status

sections.title: Patient Care Goals
sections.reference: NASEMSO National Model EMS Clinical Guidelines v3.0 — Resuscitation: Do Not Resuscitate Status/Advance Directives/Healthcare Power of Attorney (POA) Status
points: To acknowledge and maintain the variety of ways that patients can express their wishes about cardiopulmonary resuscitation or end-of-life decision making

sections.title: Patient Presentation
points: EXCLUSION CRITERIA:

Patients must have one of the following documents or a valid alternative (such as identification bracelet indicating wishes) immediately available. Note that some specifics can vary widely from state to state: a. Physician Orders for Life Sustaining Treatment (POLST) or Medical Orders for Life Sustaining Treatment (MOLST) ' explicitly describes acceptable interventions for the patient in the form of medical orders, must be signed by a physician or other empowered medical clinician to be valid
One of the documents above is valid when it meets all the following criteria: a. Conforms to the state specifications for color and construction

If there is question about the validity of the form/instrument, the best course of action is to proceed with the resuscitation until additional information can be obtained to clarify the best course of action
If a patient has a valid version of one of the above documents, it will be referred to as a 'valid exclusion to resuscitation' for the purposes of this protocol

sections.title: EMR Interventions
sections.reference: NASEMSO v3.0 — EMR level (Emergency Medical Responder)
points: Scene safety and PPE; primary survey (ABCDE)
Oxygen via NRM targeting SpO2 94-98% if indicated
Position of comfort; hemorrhage control as needed
CPR/AED if patient becomes pulseless
Epinephrine auto-injector for anaphylaxis if applicable
Naloxone intranasal unit-dose for suspected opioid involvement

sections.title: EMT Interventions
sections.reference: NASEMSO v3.0 — EMT level (Emergency Medical Technician)
points: Blood glucose monitoring; oral glucose if hypoglycemic and alert
12-lead ECG acquisition and transmission
CPAP for respiratory distress
Aspirin for suspected ischemic chest pain
Albuterol SVN for bronchospasm component
IV access maintenance (non-medicated)

sections.title: AEMT Interventions
points: IV/IO access initiation
IV/IO fluid resuscitation as indicated
Waveform capnography
IV analgesics (fentanyl, morphine) per pain management protocol
IV antiemetics (ondansetron) per protocol
IV D50W/D10W for hypoglycemia

sections.title: Paramedic Interventions
points: 12-lead ECG interpretation
Endotracheal intubation for airway compromise
RSI per protocol
Advanced antidysrhythmic or hemodynamic support per specific protocol
All parenteral medications per protocol
Vasopressors for refractory hemodynamic compromise

sections.title: Patient Safety Considerations
points: In cases where the patient's status is unclear and the appropriateness of withholding resuscitation efforts is questioned, EMS personnel should initiate CPR immediately and contact medical direction

sections.title: Educational Pearls
points: If there is a personal physician present at the scene who has an ongoing relationship with the patient, that physician may decide if resuscitation is to be initiated

If there is a registered nurse from a home healthcare or hospice agency present at the scene who has an ongoing relationship with the patient and who is operating under orders from the patient's private physician, that nurse (authorized nurse) may decide if resuscitation is to be initiated
If the physician or nurse decides resuscitation is to be initiated, usual medical direction procedures will be followed
Special Consideration: For scene safety and/or family wishes, the EMS clinician may decide to implement CPR even if all the criteria for death are met

sections.title: Key Documentation
points: ' Detailed description of the valid exclusion to resuscitation documentation used to guide resuscitation including a copy of the document if possible
' Names/contact information for significant bystanders

EMR: Scene safety, primary survey, CPR, AED, BVM, bleeding control, oral airway, O2 therapy, epinephrine auto-injector for anaphylaxis, naloxone unit-dose auto-injector
EMT: EMR scope PLUS: NPA, supraglottic airway, CPAP, pulse oximetry, 12-lead ECG acquisition, oral glucose, aspirin, patient own nitroglycerin, albuterol SVN, IM epinephrine, blood glucose monitoring, cervical collar, traction splint
AEMT: EMT scope PLUS: waveform capnography, IO access, IV access initiation, IV/IO fluids, IV medications (dextrose, naloxone, epinephrine, glucagon, analgesics, antiemetics), subcutaneous/intranasal medications, nitroglycerin from stock
Paramedic: AEMT scope PLUS: ET intubation, RSI, cricothyrotomy, needle decompression, manual defibrillation, cardioversion, transcutaneous pacing, 12-lead ECG interpretation, all antidysrhythmics, vasopressors, all medication routes and classes

deterioration_flags: In cases where the patient's status is unclear and the appropriateness of withholding resuscitation efforts is questioned, EMS personnel should initiate CPR immediately and contact medical direction
scope_notes: EMR scope is the minimum national standard — state/MCA may expand
All interventions subject to local medical director credentialing
Pediatric patients: use weight-based dosing and age-appropriate equipment
Contact medical direction when required by local protocol or for interventions outside standard scope
